commentaries.author: Cyprian of Carthage
commentaries.content: 1 Thessalonians 2:12 That you would walk worthy of God, who has called you unto his kingdom and glory. Cyprian of Carthage For what could happen to me more desirable and more joyful than to be now close to you, that you might embrace me with those hands, which, pure and innocent, and maintaining the faith of the Lord, have rejected the profane obedience. What more pleasant and sublime than now to kiss your lips, which with a glorious voice have confessed the Lord, to be looked upon even in presence by your eyes, which, despising the world, have become worthy.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: For it is our duty so to walk in the Lord's discipline as is "worthy"
